behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between manifest validation and plugin execution. A unit test of the validator alone can miss a real bug where the loader still imports/executes plugin code despite validation failure.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary plugin directory with one plugin whose manifest omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel file at import time. Run the real loader against that directory. Assert: (1) loader reports manifest rejection, and (2) sentinel file was never created, proving no plugin code executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: The real risk is in handoff between manifest parsing, dependency graph resolution, and hook invocation order across plugins. Testing only sorting logic in isolation can miss loader wiring/order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real test plugins on disk: A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory/event log exposed by the test harness. Execute the loader and assert call sequence is exactly ["B","A"].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection may be unit-testable, but this behavior also requires verifying loader behavior (neither plugin loads) after detection. That cross-module control flow is the real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A, with entry modules/onLoad writing distinct sentinels if run. Run the loader and assert: (1) cycle error includes A/B relationship, (2) neither plugin onLoad ran, and (3) no entry-module sentinel executed.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is a sequencing contract between module import execution and lifecycle hook dispatch. A unit test of hook dispatcher alone would miss real ordering regressions in loader/module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Implement a plugin whose entry module sets a module-level flag as a side effect during import; onLoad asserts that flag is already set and records success. Run loader with this plugin and assert onLoad observed the initialized flag (and no ordering assertion failed).

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on interaction between error handling, dependency graph filtering, and iterative initialization. Unit tests of try/catch logic alone do not validate real continuation semantics across plugins.
behaviors.test_description: Set up three plugins: F (throws in onLoad), I (independent), and D (depends on F). Run loader and assert: (1) F failure is reported, (2) I onLoad still runs, (3) D onLoad does not run due to dependency on failed F, and (4) process completes without aborting all plugin initialization.
